description:
You are a mobile build-engineer who must diagnose sporadic network-glitches that
    break CI/CD jobs.  
    Create a _single, fully-automated_ diagnostic bundle under  
        /home/user/diagnostics  

    STEP-BY-STEP REQUIREMENTS
    1.  Make sure the directory /home/user/diagnostics exists.  
    2.  Write a POSIX-compatible shell script named  
            /home/user/diagnostics/run_net_checks.sh  
        that, when executed, performs the following operations IN PARALLEL for
        every host in the list  
            • 127.0.0.1  
            • localhost  

        a. ping :      run «ping -n -c 1 HOST»  
        b. traceroute : run «traceroute -n -m 5 HOST»  
        c. dig :        run «dig +short HOST A»  

        For each command you must  
            • capture its complete STDOUT and STDERR,  
            • wrap the captured text between BEGIN/END marker lines (exactly as
              shown below), and  
            • append those wrapped blocks to the single log file
                  /home/user/diagnostics/netcheck.log  

        Format of every wrapped block (case-sensitive, no extra spaces):
            ===== START host=HOSTNAME check=COMMAND =====
            (zero‐or‐more lines of command output)
            ===== END host=HOSTNAME check=COMMAND =====

        Example for localhost-ping (your real output will differ):
            ===== START host=localhost check=ping =====
            PING localhost (127.0.0.1) 56(84) bytes of data.
            --- localhost ping statistics ---
            1 packets transmitted, 1 received, 0% packet loss, time 0ms
            ===== END host=localhost check=ping =====

        All six command blocks (2 hosts × 3 commands) must be written by the
        background jobs into the SAME log file without interleaving text from
        different jobs.  Use proper file locks or temporary files if necessary.

    3.  Prepend two header lines to netcheck.log (if they do not exist already):
            # Netcheck Report
            Timestamp: ISO-8601-UTC-STAMP
        The timestamp must be the ISO-8601 wall-clock time (UTC) at script start,
        for example «2025-04-01T16:30:22Z».

    4.  After writing the log, the script shall compute a SHA-256 checksum of
        /home/user/diagnostics/netcheck.log and store the hex digest only
        (no filename, no spaces, no newline after it) in  
            /home/user/diagnostics/netcheck.sha256

    5.  Make the script executable by the current user.

    HOW THE AUTOMATED TEST WILL CHECK YOUR WORK
    • The file /home/user/diagnostics/run_net_checks.sh must exist and be
      executable.  
    • When the test executes that script once, the following must hold:
      1. /home/user/diagnostics/netcheck.log exists.  
      2. Its first two lines match exactly:
            # Netcheck Report
            Timestamp: <anything>        (but must be ISO-8601-UTC pattern)  
      3. It contains all twelve marker lines (six BEGIN + six END) for every
         host/command combination shown above.  
      4. No BEGIN/END pairs are missing or duplicated.  
      5. /home/user/diagnostics/netcheck.sha256 exists and its content is a
         64-character lowercase hexadecimal string that equals the SHA-256 digest
         of the current netcheck.log file.

    NOTE
    • Do NOT hard-code the command outputs; actually execute the diagnostics.
    • Use «dig» (from dnsutils/bind-utils) for the DNS query.  It is available
      inside the evaluation container.  
    • Make sure concurrent jobs cannot corrupt the log (avoid race conditions).
    • Absolute paths must be used in the script, as the CI environment may call
      it from an arbitrary working directory.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth data the grader will use -->
    <hosts>
        <host>127.0.0.1</host>
        <host>localhost</host>
    </hosts>

    <!-- List of BEGIN and END marker lines that MUST appear in netcheck.log -->
    <requiredMarkers>
        <marker>===== START host=127.0.0.1 check=ping =====</marker>
        <marker>===== END host=127.0.0.1 check=ping =====</marker>

        <marker>===== START host=127.0.0.1 check=traceroute =====</marker>
        <marker>===== END host=127.0.0.1 check=traceroute =====</marker>

        <marker>===== START host=127.0.0.1 check=dig =====</marker>
        <marker>===== END host=127.0.0.1 check=dig =====</marker>

        <marker>===== START host=localhost check=ping =====</marker>
        <marker>===== END host=localhost check=ping =====</marker>

        <marker>===== START host=localhost check=traceroute =====</marker>
        <marker>===== END host=localhost check=traceroute =====</marker>

        <marker>===== START host=localhost check=dig =====</marker>
        <marker>===== END host=localhost check=dig =====</marker>
    </requiredMarkers>

    <!-- Header lines that must appear at top of netcheck.log -->
    <headerLine1># Netcheck Report</headerLine1>
    <headerLine2Prefix>Timestamp:</headerLine2Prefix>

    <!-- Path constants -->
    <scriptPath>/home/user/diagnostics/run_net_checks.sh</scriptPath>
    <logPath>/home/user/diagnostics/netcheck.log</logPath>
    <checksumPath>/home/user/diagnostics/netcheck.sha256</checksumPath>

    <!-- SHA-256 digest must be 64 lowercase hex chars -->
    <checksumRegex>^[0-9a-f]{64}$</checksumRegex>
